summary: zenith-utils ships postinstall dropper that fetches and executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com
details: Package masquerades as a nodemailer fork (main=lib/nodemailer.js, author and description copied from the legitimate project) but ships a postinstall dropper. package.json's postinstall runs lib/utils/index.js, which spawns a detached node process executing lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js. That file infinite-loops a GET against https://jsonkeeper.com/b/WDH3V and passes the response's `cookie` field directly into `new Function('require',...)(require)`, executing attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node privileges. Because the child is detached, the dropper survives `npm install` exit and continues fetching/executing whatever the paste host serves. lib/utils/smtp-connection/parse.js additionally exports `getCallers`, which AES-256-CBC-decrypts a hex blob using a hardcoded key and IV — a pre-staged decryptor for encrypted payloads delivered through the same channel. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste host; the package author can swap the executed code at any time without republishing. Any machine that runs `npm install zenith-utils` is fully compromised at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
